skill.name: stable-baselines3
skill.description: Guides reinforcement learning work with Stable-Baselines3, including agent training, algorithm selection, custom Gymnasium environments, callbacks, vectorized environments, and evaluation workflows.
skill.summary: Build, train, evaluate, and tune reinforcement learning agents with Stable-Baselines3.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most are false positives from Markdown code fences, RL algorithm names, hyperparameter names, documentation links, and local example code. The remaining real risk is medium: helper scripts load local Stable-Baselines3 model artifacts and create output directories, which is legitimate for RL work but should be used only with trusted files and paths.

medium_findings.title: Model Artifact Loading Requires Trusted Files
medium_findings.description: The evaluation template loads Stable-Baselines3 model files and optional VecNormalize statistics from caller-provided local paths. This is expected for RL evaluation, but users should not load untrusted model artifacts because ML model deserialization can execute unsafe payloads in some dependency stacks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly loads local model and normalization files from parameters. This is normal for SB3 workflows, but the trust boundary is real when files come from unknown sources.

medium_findings.title: Parameterized Local Output Directories
medium_findings.description: Training and evaluation helpers create log, model, evaluation, and video directories from function parameters. This is legitimate local filesystem behavior, but callers should keep paths within project workspaces to avoid unexpected writes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are direct and intentional, but they only create expected RL output folders and save model artifacts. No deletion, secret access, or exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The large external command count comes from Markdown inline code and fenced Python examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. No evidence found of runtime shell execution in the inspected skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas are documentation and code examples using Markdown formatting. The actual Python scripts reviewed do not call subprocess, os.system, eval, or exec.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto, C2, and Certificate Findings Are Scanner Noise
low_findings.description: The high-severity blocker hits map to reinforcement learning terms such as A2C, DQN, gamma, key names in dictionaries, render metadata, and TensorBoard metric names. These are domain terms, not cryptography, command-and-control behavior, or certificate handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear inside RL documentation or debug examples. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, credential files, or C2 network logic.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Findings Are Benign
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to official Stable-Baselines3, Gymnasium, TensorBoard, Python documentation, or related RL resources. They are references in Markdown, not network calls made by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed under additional resources and are not used by executable code. No HTTP client or network request behavior was found in scripts.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported dynamic import location is a normal static Python import block for Stable-Baselines3 callbacks. No evidence found of importlib, dynamic module names, or user-controlled imports.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code imports fixed modules at top level. There is no dynamic import expression or runtime import target.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local ML Model Deserialization Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script demonstrates loading saved SB3 models and normalization files from local paths. This should be treated as a trusted-input operation and documented as unsafe for untrusted artifacts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is semantically clear from the script, but exploitability depends on dependency behavior and whether the user supplies untrusted artifact files.

content.value_statement: Reinforcement learning projects often fail because environment design, algorithm choice, and evaluation details are inconsistent. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured SB3 guidance, templates, and workflow checks.
actual_capabilities: Selects SB3 algorithms for discrete, continuous, goal-conditioned, and general RL tasks.
Provides training patterns for PPO, SAC, DQN, TD3, DDPG, A2C, and HER workflows.
Explains custom Gymnasium environment structure, spaces, reset, step, render, and validation requirements.
Guides callback setup for evaluation, checkpointing, early stopping, progress tracking, and custom metrics.
Covers vectorized environments, normalization, frame stacking, multiprocessing, and evaluation differences.
Includes template scripts for training, evaluation, video recording, and custom environment scaffolding.
limitations: Does not train agents by itself unless the user runs or adapts the included scripts.
Requires installed Python dependencies such as Stable-Baselines3, Gymnasium, PyTorch, and optional extras.
Does not guarantee a policy will converge without environment-specific reward and hyperparameter tuning.
Model files and VecNormalize files should come from trusted sources only.

use_cases.title: Prototype an RL Agent
use_cases.description: Choose an SB3 algorithm, create a training loop, add evaluation callbacks, and save reproducible checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Build a Custom Gymnasium Environment
use_cases.description: Define action spaces, observation spaces, reward logic, rendering, and validation for an environment that works with SB3.

use_cases.title: Improve Training Throughput
use_cases.description: Apply vectorized environments, normalization, and callback patterns to make experiments faster and easier to compare.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Algorithm
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a Stable-Baselines3 algorithm for my environment. The action space is [discrete or continuous], observations are [describe], rewards are [describe], and my compute limit is [limit].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Training Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a Stable-Baselines3 training plan for [environment]. Include the algorithm, policy type, callbacks, evaluation frequency, logging path, checkpoint plan, and key hyperparameters to tune.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Custom Environment
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Gymnasium environment design for SB3 compatibility. Check reset, step, observation_space, action_space, rewards, termination, truncation, rendering, and check_env issues.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a Full RL Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my SB3 experiment results and propose fixes. Consider reward scaling, algorithm fit, vectorized environments, normalization, callbacks, evaluation design, seeds, and model persistence.

output_examples.input: I have a continuous-control task with dense rewards and limited samples.
output_examples.output: A recommendation to start with SAC, use normalized observations, run periodic evaluation, and compare against TD3 if policy updates appear unstable.

output_examples.input: My custom environment fails during SB3 training.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering Gymnasium return signatures, matching observation shapes, valid action spaces, reward type, terminal conditions, and check_env validation.

output_examples.input: Training is slow with a simple environment.
output_examples.output: Guidance to compare DummyVecEnv and SubprocVecEnv, tune n_envs, adjust evaluation frequency, and avoid multiprocessing overhead for lightweight environments.

best_practices: Validate every custom environment with check_env before long training runs.
Use evaluation callbacks and fixed seeds so experiment results are easier to compare.
Save normalization statistics with models when using VecNormalize.
anti_patterns: Loading model artifacts from unknown sources without reviewing trust and provenance.
Changing reward functions and hyperparameters together without recording experiment context.
Using many subprocess environments before measuring whether multiprocessing overhead helps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the marketplace skill format.

faq.question: Does it include runnable scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes templates for training, evaluation, video recording, and custom Gymnasium environments.

faq.question: Which algorithms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers PPO, SAC, DQN, TD3, DDPG, A2C, HER, and algorithm selection tradeoffs.

faq.question: Can it design custom environments?
faq.answer: It can guide environment structure, spaces, rewards, rendering, registration, and SB3 validation.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains documentation links, but the included scripts do not make network requests.

faq.question: What security caution applies?
faq.answer: Use trusted model files and keep script output paths inside project directories.
